string-functions.label: String Functions (TRIM, UPPER, LOWER)
string-functions.summary: Clean and transform text data with string functions.

sketch1.title: Why Use TRIM, UPPER, and LOWER?

sk1.title: Introduction to String Functions
sk1.bodyMarkdown:
SQL provides several string functions to help you clean and transform text data. The most common are:

- **TRIM**: Removes leading and trailing spaces from a string.
- **UPPER**: Converts all characters in a string to uppercase.
- **LOWER**: Converts all characters in a string to lowercase.

These functions are useful for standardizing data and preparing it for analysis or matching.

sk2.title: Why Use TRIM, UPPER, and LOWER?
sk2.bodyMarkdown: Data often comes in with inconsistent formatting. For example, names might have extra spaces or inconsistent capitalization. Using TRIM, UPPER, and LOWER helps ensure your queries are accurate and your results are reliable.

q1.title: Purpose of the TRIM Function
q1.prompt: What does the TRIM function do in SQL?
q1.hint: Think about spaces at the start and end of a string.

help.concept: The TRIM function is used to remove unwanted spaces at the beginning and end of a string, which is common in messy data.
help.hint_1: TRIM does not remove all spaces, only those at the start and end.
help.hint_2: If a string has spaces before or after the main text, TRIM will remove them, but spaces inside the text remain.

options.a: Removes all spaces from a string
options.b: Removes leading and trailing spaces from a string

q2.title: Selecting String Functions
q2.prompt: Which of the following SQL functions can be used to standardize the case of text data?
q2.hint: Focus on functions that change letter capitalization.

help.concept: UPPER and LOWER are used to convert text to all uppercase or all lowercase, which helps standardize data for comparison.
help.hint_1: TRIM and LENGTH do not change the case of letters.
help.hint_2: UPPER and LOWER are the functions that affect capitalization.

q3.title: Order of String Cleaning Steps
q3.prompt: Arrange the steps to standardize a name column for comparison.

help.concept: It's best to clean data (remove spaces, standardize case) before performing comparisons to ensure accuracy.
help.hint_2: After cleaning spaces, standardize the case, then compare.

q4.title: Choosing the Right Function
q4.prompt: To convert all letters in a column to uppercase, use the ____ function.
q4.hint: Which function changes all letters to capital letters?

help.concept: the missing term is the SQL function that changes all characters in a string to uppercase.
help.hint_2: The function's name suggests converting to uppercase.

q4.template: To convert all letters in a column to uppercase, use the ____ function.

q5.title: Cleaning Customer Names
q5.prompt: Write a SQL query to select the 'name' column from the 'customers_cleanup' table, removing leading and trailing spaces and converting all names to lowercase. Name the output column 'clean_name'.
q5.hint: Use both TRIM and LOWER on the 'name' column.

help.concept: Combine string functions by nesting them. TRIM removes spaces, LOWER changes case. Use AS to rename the output column.
help.hint_1: Start with TRIM to remove spaces, then apply LOWER.
help.hint_2: Wrap the TRIM function inside LOWER, and use AS to name the column 'clean_name'.
